title: Elements of Game Design
creator: Robert Zubek
description: An introductory textbook organized around mechanics and systems, gameplay, and player experience, with tools for analyzing and constructing designs.
useful: A more compact starting point for readers who find larger game-design textbooks difficult to enter.
audience: Beginning designers looking for a structured introduction to mechanics, systems, and experience.
learning: Separate mechanics, gameplay, and player experience
Analyze how systems produce patterns of play
Turn design ideas into testable prototypes
